assertions.text: [rubric-1] Classifies this as elevated risk due to breaking interface or API changes
assertions.evidence: The answer explicitly classifies the change as having 'elevated-risk categories,' specifically citing 'Breaking API changes' and 'Schema/data model changes'.

assertions.text: [rubric-2] Recommends review or explicit confirmation before merging
assertions.evidence: The answer directly recommends 'Get review first' and states that it is 'not an auto-merge candidate'.

assertions.text: [rubric-3] Explains why breaking changes are elevated risk
assertions.evidence: The answer explains that the change is risky because it 'changes the API contract' and affects 'shared types with many consumers' (CLI and API entry points).
